hook: Resale landed is still trading — but the price gap is widening fast

slides.sub: Brand new vs resale this week — who is still selling, where, and at what discount to peak.

slides.lead: Resale landed deals are closing — but sellers are giving ground on price.

slides.pre: Coral Island detached — Feb 2025 transacted PSF
slides.post: vs S$23,790 psf at Feb 2023 peak — same street, similar land area (737 sqm both deals)

slides.title: Three things the data is telling us

items.body: Sentosa Cove detached fell from S$17.4M (Feb 2023) to S$14.8M (Feb 2025) — a S$2.6M giveback on the same street.
Faber Hills semi-Ds are also softening: S$8M (Feb 2023) → S$7.1M (Jan 2025), off ~11% in 24 months.
Springwood terrace (D05 RCR) bucked the trend — Mar 2026 print of S$5.58M beats Jun 2022's S$5.03M by 11%.

slides.title: Resale landed deals — latest by district

slides.label: What the gap means
slides.punch: Coral Island gave back 15% in 24 months. Faber Hills semi-Ds shed ~11%. But D05 freehold terraces on Jalan Mat Jambol are printing higher than 2022. The market isn't falling uniformly — it's bifurcating by tenure, location, and seller urgency.
slides.punch_bold: The deals getting done are the ones where sellers accept the new reality.

slides.title: Where deals are still closing in 2025–26

items.body: Faber Hills (D05 OCR): 4 semi-D deals since Apr 2024, range S$6.88M–S$8.39M — sellers who priced right transacted within 90 days.
Springwood (D05 RCR): 5 terrace deals since Jul 2025, S$3.9M–S$6.2M — freehold terraces absorbing demand from D09/D10 priced-out buyers.
Coral Island (D04 CCR): only 1 deal since Jan 2024 — thin volume signals buyers are waiting for further price concessions.

slides.pre: Looking at landed in D04 / D05 / D10?
slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call. No pitch — just the data, the discount ranges, and the angles worth acting on.

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone weighing resale vs new landed this quarter, send them this.

caption:
📉 Resale landed is still trading — but sellers are giving up serious ground.

• Coral Island detached: S$14.8M in Feb 2025 vs S$17.4M at peak — a S$2.6M giveback
• Faber Hills semi-Ds off ~11% in 24 months; thin Sentosa volume signals more to come
• D05 freehold terraces bucking the trend — Springwood Mar 2026 beat 2022 prints by 11%

The market isn't falling uniformly — it's bifurcating by tenure, location, and seller urgency. The deals closing are where sellers accept the new reality first.
